title: apicra deltoidea var. turgida
intro: Apicra deltoidea var. turgida, also known as the Mexican prickly poppy, is a plant species native to Mexico and the southwestern United States, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and prickly stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and other compounds. The health benefits of Apicra deltoidea var. turgida include relief from pain, fever reduction, and treatment of respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
